object_requirements.text: Every canonical object MUST carry stable identity, type, schema version, creation provenance, purpose/authority where applicable, access class, review or expiry, revision and audit references.
Canonical identifiers MUST be stable, opaque and free of unnecessary sensitive meaning.
Identifiers and public references MUST NOT embed diagnoses, crisis states, legal status, secret credentials or other sensitive attributes.
Person, organization, system component, account, device, session and role assignment MUST remain distinct.
A material object MUST identify affected people or record why they cannot yet be identified.
Knowledge and event objects MUST distinguish speaker or generator, subject, source and affected people.
A role assignment MUST identify scope, purpose, authority, permissions, prohibitions, conflict and review or expiry.
Representative authority MUST use a distinct evidence-linked, scoped, expiring and appealable object.
Personal-data, interpretive and consequential objects MUST reference an active declared purpose or documented exception.
Consent, requested service, safety, mandatory, security, research and disputed bases MUST remain distinguishable.
Participation MUST be represented per purpose/decision and MUST NOT become a maturity, compliance or worth score.
Observation, statement, imported record, inference, hypothesis, pattern, judgment, recommendation, decision and action MUST remain distinguishable.
A derived object MUST preserve source and transformation lineage subject to privacy and safety controls.
Material unknowns, alternatives and dissent MUST be representable without flattening them into one confidence number.
Event, relationship and context objects MUST preserve time and perspective without automatic causal claims.
A high-impact decision MUST reference affected people, evidence, uncertainty, authority, accountable human/organization, reason, review and appeal.
An action object SHOULD identify reversibility, observation plan, review condition and rollback where applicable.
Feedback and outcome objects MUST be able to trigger revision without erasing earlier provenance.
Personal and derived data MUST be inventoried with purpose, authority, location, access, retention, backup and rights routes.
Memory MUST reference sources, current use, visibility, retention, correction, restriction, deletion and supersession.
Rights requests MUST support applicable access, explanation, correction, contextualization, restriction, objection, export, deletion, withdrawal, high-impact review and appeal.
A retention profile or review interval MUST NOT be represented as automatic permission to retain or use data.
A risk signal or assessment MUST NOT silently become a permanent identity or unrestricted profile.
A handoff object MUST distinguish initiated, acknowledged, accepted, failed, redirected and closed states.
A resource record MUST carry verification, expiry, accessibility, scope, owner and stale behavior.
Incident and appeal objects MUST preserve timeline, affected people, conflicts, evidence, decisions, remediation and closure authority.
An exception MUST be narrow, purpose-bound, evidence-linked, time/review-bounded, safeguarded and appealable.
Audit events MUST preserve material accountability while minimizing sensitive content and restricting access.
Current and historical state MUST remain separately queryable; supersession MUST NOT silently rewrite history.
Canonical objects and sensitive fields MUST carry an access class and minimum-necessary disclosure rule.
Unknown, not-applicable, unavailable, withheld and not-yet-collected states SHOULD remain distinguishable.
Implementations MAY add namespaced extensions when core semantics, provenance, access and migration remain intact.
A product table, prompt variable or legacy object name MUST NOT become a universal Kernel object merely because it exists.
A family, education, counseling or cultural method MUST NOT become a universal object without cross-scene admission review.
Implementations MAY use relational, graph, document, event-sourced or other storage when semantic/lifecycle obligations remain verifiable.
A breaking object change MUST define identity continuity, field migration, lineage, coexistence, rollback, affected-person impact and dependent lifecycle changes.

access_classes.name: PublicGoverned
access_classes.scope: Approved public normative/governance or aggregate content
access_classes.prohibited_default: No raw identifiable personal or restricted evidence

access_classes.name: InternalGoverned
access_classes.scope: Non-public operational/architecture records without sensitive personal detail
access_classes.prohibited_default: No public or unrelated staff access

access_classes.name: RestrictedPersonal
access_classes.scope: Personal, relationship, interpretation, memory and rights records
access_classes.prohibited_default: No marketing, unrestricted analytics or general model training

access_classes.name: RestrictedSafety
access_classes.scope: Risk, safeguarding, safety action, incident and protected-source records
access_classes.access_rule: Need-to-know safety roles with independent review

access_classes.name: RestrictedEvidence
access_classes.scope: Review, validation, qualification and identity evidence
access_classes.prohibited_default: No public repository, sales, personalization or unrelated reuse

access_classes.name: SecretCredential
access_classes.scope: Secrets, tokens, private keys and authentication material

lifecycle_requirements.text: Every transition MUST record lifecycle/version, object, previous/new state, actor/component, role, authority, purpose, reason, evidence, uncertainty, affected people, time, review/expiry and audit.
A material object MUST NOT change state without an append-oriented transition record.
Current state and transition history MUST remain separately inspectable.
An implementation MUST reject or explicitly exception-record a transition not allowed by the applicable lifecycle.
A material transition MUST identify responsible actor, role and authority scope.
An AI component MUST NOT be the final accountable authorizer of a high-impact transition.
Active authority, participation, knowledge, resource, exception and safety states MUST support review or expiry.
A lifecycle state MUST NOT become a permanent identity, maturity score, diagnosis or worth judgment.
Restrictive, emergency, disputed or irreversible transitions MUST preserve reason, evidence, uncertainty, alternatives and safeguards.
A materially affected person SHOULD receive usable notice unless a specific temporary safety/legal reason is recorded.
Material lifecycles MUST provide applicable correction, contestation, appeal or independent-review transitions.
Where reversal is impossible, the transition MUST record remaining exposure and remediation.
Expired, revoked, disputed or unsafe authority MUST freeze expansion, optional reuse and high-impact action unless narrowly excepted.
Contested high-impact knowledge SHOULD NOT drive irreversible, punitive, rights-restricting or identity-shaping action during review.
Revocation, expiry or supersession of authority MUST propagate to dependent optional permissions and actions.
Disputed or conflicted representative authority MUST route high-impact action to conflict-free review.
Before reducing direct participation, the system MUST consider communication and decision support.
Declined, withdrawn, silent or inaccessible participation MUST NOT be treated as agreement or proof.
A contested knowledge item MUST expose dispute and dependent uses and support restrict, correct, contextualize or supersede.
Supersession MUST update active uses while preserving prior provenance.
A high-impact decision MUST pass impact, affected-person, authority, evidence, uncertainty, reversibility and review gates.
Executed action MUST support outcome, feedback and revision transitions.
Deletion/restriction MUST NOT become Complete while prohibited active use remains possible.
Backup completion MUST define restoration-time suppression and reconciliation.
An open exception MUST carry owner, scope, safeguards and mandatory review or expiry.
A safety signal MUST be assessed with current/historical evidence, uncertainty, immediacy and responsible reviewer.
Initiated or acknowledged handoff MUST NOT be represented as accepted or closed without evidence.
Failed handoff MUST identify next action, owner and transparent limitation.
Temporary protective action MUST be minimum necessary, time-bounded where possible, independently reviewable and correctable.
An incident MUST NOT close before containment, investigation, remediation, verification, affected-person impact and closure authority are addressed or bounded.
A material appeal MUST use a different or conflict-free reviewer where the original role is conflicted.
A stale, disputed or expired resource MUST stop being presented as current.
Every transition MUST identify the state-machine version used.
A breaking lifecycle revision MUST define state mapping, in-flight handling, rollback, audit continuity and affected-person notice.

ambiguities.issue: Which concepts require universal objects rather than RFC/Profile objects?
How identity linkage supports correction without a universal identity database.
How collective, relational, future and non-user affected people are represented.
Which role combinations are absolute or conditional conflicts.
Which basis categories are universal versus jurisdiction-specific.
How lifecycle states relate to P0–P5 without hierarchy.
How context supports understanding without surveillance.
When observation becomes inference, hypothesis, pattern or judgment.
Whether numeric confidence is appropriate and interpretable.
How subjects inspect evidence without exposing protected sources.
How outcome and pattern records prevent causal overclaim.
Which objects and transitions require high-impact review.
What information, authority and independence make accountability meaningful.
How feedback avoids retaliation or adverse identity labels.
What retained derived state is Memory versus transient context or audit.
How far corrections propagate across generated/shared records.
What audit may remain after deletion without recreating a profile.
When immutable backup handling is sufficient.
How risk states avoid permanent identity or broad access.
Which transitions may occur before notice or direct participation.
What proves acknowledgment, acceptance and responsibility transfer.
How local/minority evidence changes resource status.
Which incident information is visible to affected people and reporters.
What counts as a conflict-free reviewer in a small team.
Who approves exceptions and which requirements are non-exceptionable.
How audit correction and privacy rights coexist.
Whether field-level access classes are required.
How unknown, withheld, unavailable and not-applicable remain usable.
How extensions remain interoperable and cannot bypass rights.
How Family OS objects map without becoming authority.
How object/state semantics remain traceable in Chinese and English.
How implementations remain understandable without collapsing distinctions.
